evals.name: direct-go-no-go
evals.prompt: We're supposed to deploy the ecommerce-api to prod on Friday. Can you go through it and tell me if it's actually ready to ship? I want a straight go/no-go, not a maybe.
evals.expected_output: A grouped severity report with a NOT READY verdict. Flags the blank Postgres password (config, critical) and the unguarded stock oversell race in OrderService.create (concurrency, critical). Cites real file:line for each finding, groups by severity, credits clean areas, and does not wrongly flag ddl-auto=validate.
assertions: Gives a clear NOT READY / no-go verdict prominently (not a hedge)
Identifies the blank Postgres datasource password as a critical config finding
Identifies the unguarded stock read-check-write oversell race in OrderService.create as a critical concurrency finding
Every finding cites a concrete file:line location
Findings are grouped by severity (critical / warning / info)
Does NOT wrongly flag ddl-auto=validate as a production risk (validate is correct for prod)
Credits at least one genuinely clean area as passed (DTO boundary, @Transactional coverage, or input validation)

evals.name: pre-launch-implicit
evals.prompt: before this goes live i want to make sure nothing embarrassing is gonna blow up on us — like leaked passwords, some race condition that lets people order stuff we don't have, that kind of thing. can you audit the codebase and lay out what needs fixing first?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as a production readiness audit without the exact phrase. Finds the blank DB password and the stock oversell race as top-priority criticals, ordered by severity, in the grouped report format with file:line citations.
assertions: Produces a structured severity-graded readiness report (recognizes the intent despite no explicit 'production readiness' phrase)
Identifies the blank Postgres datasource password as a critical config finding
Identifies the unguarded stock read-check-write oversell race in OrderService.create as a critical concurrency finding
Lays out what to fix first, ordered by severity (criticals before warnings)
Every finding cites a concrete file:line location
Does NOT wrongly flag ddl-auto=validate as a production risk (validate is correct for prod)

evals.name: hardening-review-then-fix
evals.prompt: Do a hardening pass over this Spring Boot API before release — config, transactions, the web layer, concurrency, all of it. Give me the report first, then I'll tell you what to fix.
evals.expected_output: Systematic report covering all five areas with a go/no-go verdict up top. Catches the config secret and the concurrency race as criticals, lists N+1 / validation warnings, shows a Passed section, and offers to fix in severity order without editing code yet.
assertions: Report addresses all five areas: config, transactions, web layer (dto/validation/error-handling), and concurrency
Identifies the blank Postgres datasource password as a critical config finding
Identifies the unguarded stock read-check-write oversell race in OrderService.create as a critical concurrency finding
A go/no-go verdict appears at the top of the report
Ends by offering to fix (in severity order) and does NOT edit any repository files yet — waits for the user to choose
Flags at least one N+1 query risk as a warning
